mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals a tight labor market, which typically leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE is below 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1.0%) creates a 'stagflation' risk premium, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO < 104.0) during a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals recessionary risks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 104.0 and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2.0% q/q) indicates robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 > 2.0% q/q.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals expanding employment, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST > 1800k) indicates strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST > 1800.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS > 660k) and industrial output (INDPRO > 104.5) signals broad economic strength, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS > 660k and INDPRO > 104.5.
